name: Inventivio
description: The new Tactonom Reader enables blind people to access, understand, and learn with graphics independently. The interactive system is especially suited for education because it allows users to work with the same learning formats as sighted people. This is achieved by augmenting tactile information with additional audio explanations about what you sense under your fingertips. Discover how the Tactonom Reader enables blind people to realize their full educational potential.

name: AAVAA
description: We build wearable devices that interpret the brain and bio-signals using our patented sensor technology to track users' attention, eye movement, facial gestures, and subtle commands and transform them into meaningful, actionable commands. As a result, users can operate their device voice-free, hands-free, and touch-free for the first time simply by moving their head and blinking. Many markets can benefit from this technology, including assistive technologies.
categories: AAC; Alternative Input/Keyboard Devices; Assistive Mobility; Deaf & Hard of Hearing

name: Accessibility Partners Canada
description: Accessibility Partners Canada, a leader in inclusivity solutions, presents its USA conference. Delaware-based subsidiary Accessibility Innovation Inc. showcases expertise in ADA compliance and universal design. Our services, audits, consultations, and training focus on enhancing digital and physical accessibility. We're dedicated to empowering organizations to achieve top accessibility standards, ensuring equal access for all. Join us in advancing inclusivity across various sectors worldwide.

name: Aira
description: Aira is a live, human-to-human professional assistance service for people who are blind or low vision. Using a camera and the Aira app on someone’s device of ‘choice,’ a professionally-trained Agent assists by visually interpreting what is in the camera’s view. Aira is used by residents, employees and customers of airports, banks, the largest distribution centers, entire states, software companies, financial firms, retailers and universities. Aira enhances independence, efficiency and autonomy.
categories: Assistive Mobility; Blind/Low Vision; Daily Living

name: Allyant
description: At Allyant, we believe every organization’s journey toward equitable access should be simple and seamless, eliminating the worry, stress, and uncertainty often associated with accessibility. For this reason, we offer many accessibility services and software solutions, spanning print and digital document remediation services, document remediation software, and digital accessibility auditing.

name: Amazon
description: Amazon's vision is to be Earth's most customer-centric company. Our goal isn't simply to make things accessible but to make products, services, and experiences that are delightful for customers with disabilities. Customers can use Alexa to identify household items, Fire tablets with Bluetooth refreshable braille displays, Kindle's customizable reading experience, Fire TV accessibility features, including the VoiceView screen reader, audio support for compatible hearing aids, and more.
categories: Blind/Low Vision; Daily Living; Deaf & Hard of Hearing; Software; Telecommunication Devices

name: Ambutech Inc.
description: Ambutech leads the way in innovative mobility solutions, empowering you to navigate the world with confidence and ease. Our canes and mobility devices are crafted with precision and care. We understand that everyone's journey is unique. That's why Ambutech offers a diverse range of products, from customizable canes to tips and accessories for all your needs. Whatever your lifestyle, we have the perfect companion for you.
categories: Assistive Mobility; Blind/Low Vision; Daily Living

name: American Printing House for the Blind
description: At the American Printing House for the Blind (APH), we believe in empowering blind or visually impaired people by providing accessible and innovative products, materials, and services for lifelong success. Our vision is for an accessible future with opportunities for everyone.
categories: Blind/Low Vision; Daily Living; Educational Software

name: American Thermoform Corporation / Braillo / Zychem
description: American Thermoform is the parent company of Braillo Norway and Zychem Limited, and a leading manufacturer and distributor of Production Braille Embossers, Braille Paper, Thermoform Duplicators, Brailon, Swell Form Machines, Swell Touch Paper, Braille Labels, and more. Braillo is recognized as the global industry leader in production braille embossers and Zychem in tactile graphics - stop by and see why. www.americanthermoform.com, www.braillo.com, www.zychemltd.com.

name: Another Day Co., Ltd.
description: Another Day is a company that develops various cultural and artistic content to improve low cultural accessibility for the disabled and socially vulnerable.

name: Ashirase, Inc.
description: We provide "ASHIRASE," a walking navigation system using an IoT device for people with low vision, available on iOS.
categories: Assistive Mobility; Blind/Low Vision; Cognitive; Daily Living

name: Audible Sight
description: Make the world more accessible, one video at a time. Audible Sight is the first-ever audio description solution for the general public. No technical skills or experience is required. Audible Sight leverages AI technology and lifelike synthetic voices. Now, anyone can create audio descriptions for their videos. Help to bridge the gap in accessibility for the visually impaired. Try Audible Sight for free: audiblesight.ai

name: Autonomous Living Technologies
description: AULI.TECH designs open-source applications and devices enabling individuals to overcome neuromuscular and vocal impairments. We leverage complex AI and Robotics technologies running in the cloud, consumer devices, and wearable gear to create low-cost, accessible solutions.
categories: AAC; Assistive Mobility; Daily Living; Other

name: Awarewolf Gear
description:
Originally designed for the backcountry trails of Sedona, Arizona, the All-Terrain Cane (ATC) excels everywhere life takes you. Whether you're taking a stroll down the block, at the beach, on snowy sidewalks, your grandchild's playground, or on the trails, the ATC gives you the power to explore where other mobility canes wouldn't dream to go. The ATC features traditional white and red reflective materials as users are accustomed to, and much more.
Introducing the Urban Xplorer series for 2024.
categories: Assistive Mobility; Blind/Low Vision; Daily Living

name: AYES
description:
AYES provides an AI application, called oko, that helps visually impaired people navigate their environment safely and independently. Our oko app makes every traffic light accessible by detecting the traffic light and bringing that information back to the user using audio and haptic feedback.
Our oko app has helped cross more than 2 million streets to date and is growing rapidly. Join our booth 619 to learn more about the future of the oko app.

name: BarrierBreak & 247 Accessible Documents
description: BarrierBreak & 247 Accessible Documents deliver digital accessibility solutions globally. Our Digital Accessibility solutions include Accessibility Testing, Audit services & VPAT Creation. Our accessible document remediation services ensure that your PDF, Word, PowerPoint & Excel documents are accessible. As the Leaders in Offshore Digital Accessibility Testing & Consulting, we manage your costs while maintaining quality to meet WCAG Conformance & Section 508 compliance.

name: Bellman & Symfon
description:
Autonomy through technology. 

We are driven to make a difference for hard-of-hearing and deaf people. 

We realize this by offering a wide range of solutions such as smart home systems, hearing amplifiers, alarm clocks, telecom access tools, and safety devices including smoke and carbon monoxide detectors, that empower people to stay safe, independent, and socially active. 

We invite you to visit shop.bellman.com for more information and to book a demo.

name: biped.ai
description: biped is an all-in-one mobility device for people with low vision. A harness, worn on the shoulders, can detect obstacles and holes, describe the surroundings with AI, and provide GPS navigation instructions.

name: Blazie Technologies LLC
description: BT Speak, a Braille pocket computer.

name: Blind CAN Film Festival
description: The Blind Can Film Festival features the stories of people who are blind and the tools we utilize to get to work and play, our featured presentation is the Blind Canvas Project, combining amazing works of art created from the minds of storytellers who are blind as part of a feature film by blind Filmmaker Ben Fox.

name: BlindShell USA
description:
We've created the world's best all-in-one accessible cell phone that provides greater independence, freedom to express, and the ability to communicate however you desire. It's time to join the BlindShell community and see what all the buzz is about. Physical keypad, tactile buttons, enhanced loudspeaker, menu-driven, and more.
Some of our featured essential apps are available on the BlindShell Classic 2.
Aira, Alexa, Be My AI, BARD Mobile, Google Lookout, ScripTalk, Sero, WayAround, Zoom.
categories: Alternative Input/Keyboard Devices; Assistive Mobility; Blind/Low Vision; Daily Living; Deaf & Hard of Hearing; Educational Software; Learning Disabilities; Software; Telecommunication Devices

name: HumanWare
description: HumanWare, a member of EssilorLuxottica, creates solutions empowering people with vision loss. Since 1988, HumanWare has developed assistive technologies such as video magnifiers, braille devices, audio assistants, and GPS, helping students, seniors, and professionals with visual impairments to see the world differently and gain greater autonomy.
categories: Assistive Mobility; Blind/Low Vision; Daily Living; Deaf & Hard of Hearing

name: Braille Institute of America
description: The Braille Institute is the leading agency for blind and low-vision people in southern California, with seven centers for in-person, one-on-one instruction and group classes. We now provide remote services around the world! With a mission to lead people toward independence and pursuing life goals, our programs include access technology, braille instruction, cane travel, Independent Living Skills, library services, Occupational Therapy, arts and music, and social support groups.
categories: Agencies/Services; Blind/Low Vision; Daily Living; Other

name: Brava Home, Inc.
description: The Brava Smart Oven transforms home cooking for AT users of all abilities with safe, automated technology. With guided recipes and an integrated app for unparalleled ease of use, it cooks full meals 2-4x faster (with the press of a button) using patented Pure Light Cooking™ Technology. Constant software enhancements expand capabilities, improving access and experience for the AT community.
categories: AAC; Blind/Low Vision; Cognitive; Daily Living; Deaf & Hard of Hearing; Learning Disabilities; Software

name: California School for the Blind
description: In addition to on-site educational programs, the California School for the Blind maintains an extensive outreach program that provides no-cost consultation, assessment, training, and technical assistance for educational teams and families of visually impaired students throughout the state. AT specialists are available in person, via email, online, or by phone to identify student technology needs and provide support and training. Free how-to guides are available on various software and devices.

name: California State University, Northridge - ATHS Graduate Program
description: Empower those in need with ATHS degrees from CSUN. The M.S. in Assistive Technology and Human Services program will teach the latest social, medical, legal, and practical knowledge for those who assist persons with disabilities and prepare you for a career focused on enhancing the lives of those who rely on assistive technology devices. Learn more at https://go.csun.edu/ATHS

name: Case for Vision LLC
description: At Case For Vision, we design and manufacture assistive devices for visually impaired people or those with age-related vision loss. Our mission is to offer affordable and intuitive alternatives to the expensive magnifiers currently on the market. Case For Vision and its sister company, Stand For Vision, are distributors of popular assistive devices from leading European manufacturers, such as VisioBraille and Kobavision.

name: CATT
description: CATT (Center for Assistive Technology Training) offers complimentary training in Assistive Technology for educators, parents, and students. We emphasize the "train the trainer" approach, with a special focus on products tailored for individuals with visual impairments or blindness.

name: Chax Training and Consulting
description: Document Accessibility experts Chad Chelius and Dax Castro (Chax) provide Accessibility training for Adobe and Microsoft software for businesses looking to train their teams or implement an accessibility program within their organization. Simply put, we solve document accessibility problems.

name: Clix Therapy
description: Clix - Reusable Body Treatment is a hot & cold therapy product with various uses. Clix therapy products are helpful for rehabilitation practices in minor injuries where heat therapy is directed for use. The instant heat pad can loosen and soothe muscle injuries and stiff joints. Meanwhile, the cool pad works to reduce swelling that causes inflammation.

name: Continual Engine LLC
description: Continual Engine is dedicated to building transformative accessibility and learning process automation solutions that provide high-quality, affordable, and scalable experiences. We leverage award-winning technologies like PREP, Invicta, and other proprietary tools to automate, fast-track, and bring exponential cost savings to our clients for accessible documents, images, videos, courses, and more. Contact us to learn how we can help accessibility professionals do more with less.
categories: Agencies/Services; Blind/Low Vision; Cognitive; Educational Software; Learning Disabilities; Software

name: Convo
description:
Founded in March 2009, Convo is the world’s largest Deaf-owned business with over 500 employees serving five countries in ten different languages.

At Convo, we believe that conversations are the key to enhancing the lives of 70 million Deaf individuals and the people around them. By creating communication solutions to address the experience deprivation that many Deaf people face, we can have a lasting and positive impact on their daily lives.
categories: Agencies/Services; Deaf & Hard of Hearing; Telecommunication Devices

name: Crawford Technologies Inc.
description: Ensure your communications reach a wide audience and are easily understood! Our offerings enable the delivery of large volumes of documents in accessible formats such as WCAG-compliant Accessible PDF, PDF/UA, braille, Reflowable Large Print, and audio, along with high-speed language translations between over 120 languages. Say goodbye to language and communication obstacles – we empower you to connect with individuals in their preferred accessible format and language, true inclusion.

name: CViConnect
description: The development team at LifeScience Technologies, led by teachers for the visually impaired and leading CVI researchers, has designed the newly released CViConnect PRO. CViConnect PRO is an innovative system providing accessible Activities designed to meet the unique needs of learners with CVI.
categories: Blind/Low Vision; Daily Living; Educational Software; Other; Software

name: CVS Health
description: Visit the CVS Health booth to learn more about our work developing accessibility open-source software and for a hands-on demo of Spoken Rx®, a breakthrough feature on the CVS Pharmacy® app that reads prescription information aloud in English or Spanish via radio-frequency identification (RFID) technology. Spoken Rx is an important aid for patients with complete blindness, significant visual impairment, literacy or language difficulties, or dyslexia.

name: Defense Intelligence Agency - DIA
description:
Our diverse workforce is skilled in military history and doctrine, economics, physics, chemistry, world history, political science, bio-sciences, and computer sciences. We travel the world, meeting and working closely with professionals from foreign countries.
DIA provides military intelligence to warfighters, defense policymakers, and force planners in the Department of Defense and Intelligence Community in support of U.S. military planning and operations and weapon systems acquisition.

name: Described and Captioned Media Program
description: DCMP is the nation’s leading source of accessible educational videos. Families and school personnel with at least one early learner to Grade 12 student with a disability can register for free access to thousands of videos. DCMP also provides media accessibility information, guidelines for captioning and describing media, and eLearning resources for professional development.
categories: Blind/Low Vision; Deaf & Hard of Hearing; Learning Disabilities

name: Designs for Vision, Inc.
description: Designs for Vision, Inc. is a manufacturer of Low Vision devices. We offer cosmetically appealing options in telescopic and microscopic glasses that will enhance your usable vision to allow you to perform routine daily tasks like watching TV, reading, spotting signs in the distance, seeing faces, working on the computer, etc.

name: Disability Rights California
description:
Disability Rights California - Protecting and advancing the rights of individuals with disabilities across California for more than 40 years.
Our Mission: Disability Rights California (DRC) defends, advances, and strengthens the rights and opportunities of people with disabilities.

name: Dolphin Computer Access
description: Dolphin assistive technology products are designed to improve accessibility and independence on computers, tablets and phones, for people with a wide range of print disabilities. This includes screen readers for people who are blind, magnification for those with low vision and an accessible reader for those with any print impairment. Visit Dolphin at booth 418.
categories: Blind/Low Vision; Educational Software; Learning Disabilities; Software

name: Dot Incorporation
description: We are a Seoul, Korea-based company that provides never-before-seen, cutting-edge technology to grant mobility, confidence, and independence to those living with daily challenges. To solve the invisible information accessibility gap existing everywhere, Dot has self-developed an innovative and futuristic multi-actuator and applied it to its products, solving the basic problems of size, price, and functions of the existing braille devices for visually impaired people.
categories: Blind/Low Vision; Daily Living; Learning Disabilities; Telecommunication Devices

name: Dream Vision Group
description: Dream Vision Group is the North American Distributor of Handy Tech braille products by Help Tech. These are the only braille displays offering ergonomically curved braille cells and Active Tactile Control (ATC). ATC is touch-responsive braille cell technology that enables many unique features, including capturing and analyzing student reading data and creating custom reading profiles. Email info@dvg-llc.com, call 512-662-1629, or visit us at Booth 411 to learn more.

name: Case for Vision LLC
description: At Case For Vision, we design and manufacture assistive devices for visually impaired people or those with age-related vision loss. Our mission is to offer affordable and intuitive alternatives to the expensive magnifiers currently on the market. Case For Vision and its sister company, Stand For Vision, are distributors of popular assistive devices from leading European manufacturers, such as VisioBraille and Kobavision.

name: Duxbury Systems
description:
DBT 12.7 braille translation software for Windows & Mac, including mathematics and graphics support in many world languages.
eBrailler Cosmo Brailler Writer and BERT Remote Education Software.
NimPro for ePub & publisher files.
categories: Agencies/Services; Blind/Low Vision; Daily Living; Educational Software; Learning Disabilities; Other; Software

name: East Texas Lighthouse For The Blind
description: We empower the blind through rehabilitation, education, training, and employment. Our programs include regional, state, and national training.

name: Echo Labs
description: We help universities nationwide save millions on captioning. Slash your bill and get next-day delivery by default, all while maintaining ADA accuracy. CSUN attendees get a complementary and unrestricted two-week trial by texting 470-261-1343.
categories: Deaf & Hard of Hearing; Educational Software; Software

name: EMVI.AI
description: EMVI is pioneering the world's first all-in-one hands-free application that specifically meets the needs of people with visual impairment by integrating mobility safety features (e.g., obstacle detection, stairs, step hazards, drop-offs, crosswalk identification, etc.) and detailed scenery descriptions (e.g., bus and bus number detection, entrance identification, product recognition, etc.). Discover the world through EMVI's ability to bridge your last mile and enhance your quality of life.

name: Envision
description: Envision enables people who are blind or who have low vision unobtrusive access to the visual world through its award-winning smart glasses and apps. From ground-breaking AI-powered visual assistance to real-time text recognition, scene descriptions, voice controls and hands-free video calling, Envision is always innovating for inclusivity.
categories: Blind/Low Vision; Daily Living; Learning Disabilities; Software; Telecommunication Devices

name: eSight
description: eSight is the enhanced vision-platform empowering individuals with sight loss to see new possibilities. A clinically-validated Class 1 Medical Device, eSight is worn daily by thousands of people with best-corrected visual acuity of 20/60-20/800 caused by 24 different eye conditions including macular degeneration, diabetic retinopathy, and Stargardt's disease. Wearing eSight, many achieve 20/20 and retain 100% mobility. Learn more at www.eSightEyewear.com.

name: Evinced, Inc.
description: Evinced is the rapidly growing technology leader in digital accessibility software, helping enterprises make their web and mobile offerings accessible to everyone. Its AI-enabled products save developer time and reduce risk via proprietary discovery and analysis algorithms that integrate directly into software development pipelines.

name: Feeldom
description: Feeldom is a small team of designers and craftspeople from Seoul, S. Korea, who handcraft premium wheelchair bags, low-vision backpacks, and adaptive gear. We hand-pick the best materials and make everything ourselves because we believe the disability community deserves to look good and have gear engineered for the individual's lifestyle. Feeldom's award-winning designs are based 100% on user feedback from people with disabilities, so we're continually upgrading our products to serve you.
categories: Assistive Mobility; Blind/Low Vision; Daily Living; Other

name: Ghotit LTD
description: Ghotit offers desktop-based writing and reading solutions that improve productivity and writing quality. Initially designed for individuals with dyslexia and dysgraphia, these solutions have evolved to cater to diverse user needs. Ghotit's desktop application is a preferred choice for organizations with high data privacy and security concerns. Its low-cost Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) solution serves multiple users with different needs.

name: Glean
description: Glean’s personal study app creates confident learners. Students capture everything from class, stay organized, review the content at their own pace, and build personalized resources through a structured learning process. Glean for Education solves how you provide effective note-taking accommodations, improving student success and retention. More than 750 institutions use Glean to reduce stress, boost confidence, and improve grades.

name: Glidance
description: Founded by Amos Miller, who led Microsoft’s Soundscape project, Glidance is transforming personal robotics by using AI and advanced technology to solve real human needs. Working with engineers, designers, and a community of over 8,000 blind and low vision individuals, Glidance is developing Glide, the world’s first Intelligent Guide. Quick to learn, easy to use, and affordable, Glide helps people navigate more safely, confidently, and independently.
categories: Assistive Mobility; Blind/Low Vision; Cognitive; Daily Living; Deaf & Hard of Hearing; Learning Disabilities; Software

name: GoodMaps
description:
Experience the future of indoor navigation through GoodMaps’ innovative mapping technology. With its sustainable, infrastructure-free implementation – not to mention an accessible, user-friendly, and efficient interface – it provides unparalleled access and a fully immersive journey that will empower everyone to explore more precisely.
GoodMaps makes the world’s indoor spaces more inclusive, interactive, and joyful for everyone.

name: GrackleDocs
description:
GrackleDocs is a pioneering software and services company dedicated to enhancing digital accessibility. At the heart of our mission lies a commitment to ensuring that digital content is easily accessible and navigable for everyone, regardless of physical or cognitive disabilities.
We specialize in offering innovative solutions that help our customers comply with international accessibility standards, making their digital documents and other online material universally accessible.
categories: Agencies/Services; Blind/Low Vision; Cognitive; Educational Software; Software

name: GrandCare Shiftability
description: Proven to reduce staffing costs, GrandCare is a remote monitoring touch platform to help individuals and those who support them. The locked-down, personalized touchscreen provides to-do checklists, reminders, HIPAA video chat, games, videos, and more. Activity & telehealth sensors alert remote caregivers when additional support is needed. ShiftAbility is a transformation consulting group that assists agencies in making a shift to technology-first using GrandCare and the Smart Living Model.
categories: Cognitive; Daily Living; Software; Telecommunication Devices

name: Hamilton CapTel
description: When it comes to hearing on the phone, Hamilton® CapTel® can make all the difference. For individuals with hearing loss, reliable captions of what's said over the phone ensure clarity on every call. Captions are available at home, at work, and on the go on various captioned telephones, smart devices, and Cisco® business phones. For traditional Relay services, Hamilton Relay® provides Telecommunications Relay Services for the State of California.
categories: Deaf & Hard of Hearing; Telecommunication Devices

name: Handi Exceller
description:
Handi Exceller designed this product to respond to a specific problem: How to offer a learning experience that is fun, independent, and inclusive.
In partnership with a Braille Teacher, a visually impaired colleague, and an expert seeking to address this point, we developed Br’eye, an innovative braille learning solution aiming to be evolving, customizable, and adaptative. Our mission is to render the learning of braille a unique experience.
categories: Blind/Low Vision; Educational Software; Learning Disabilities

name: Harpo Sp. z o.o
description: Harpo’s mission is to create solutions for people with vision and communication impairments so that they can interact with the world, realize their ambitions, and help the world see them for the talented people they are. Our products have been delivered to all corners of the world. Our products provide the quality, innovation, and practicality our customers seek. Come to our booth and see our new product - BraillePen24 - a super-slim mobile braille notetaker.

name: HIMS, Inc.
description: Check out the new products from HIMS. HIMS, Inc. is a global manufacturer and distributor that has provided Braille devices since 1999. At HIMS Inc., innovation is driven by feedback from customers and employees. Stop by and check out our new Braille product.

name: HumanWare
description: HumanWare, a member of EssilorLuxottica, creates solutions empowering people with vision loss. Since 1988, HumanWare has developed assistive technologies such as video magnifiers, braille devices, audio assistants, and GPS, helping students, seniors, and professionals with visual impairments to see the world differently and gain greater autonomy.
categories: Assistive Mobility; Blind/Low Vision; Daily Living; Deaf & Hard of Hearing

name: IAccessible Inc.
description: IAccessible, iaccessible.net, is a disability-owned social enterprise set up to connect product makers and enterprises to people with disabilities. Trusted by partners like Adobe, SAP, the Compass Group, and others, we have years of experience driving accessibility and inclusion.
categories: Agencies/Services; Blind/Low Vision; Cognitive; Deaf & Hard of Hearing; Learning Disabilities; Software

name: Ghotit LTD
description: Ghotit offers desktop-based writing and reading solutions that improve productivity and writing quality. Initially designed for individuals with dyslexia and dysgraphia, these solutions have evolved to cater to diverse user needs. Ghotit's desktop application is a preferred choice for organizations with high data privacy and security concerns. Its low-cost Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) solution serves multiple users with different needs.

name: Index Braille
description: Braille printing made easy! Index Braille has manufactured braille embossers since 1982. Basic-D, Everest-D, BrailleBox, and FanFold-D V5 are presented at the Index Braille or HumanWare booth at CSUN. The BrailleApp supports direct printing from common operating systems in more than 150 braille languages (literary & contracted braille). Supports standard files such as doc, docx, pdf, and epub3.

name: InnoCaption
description: InnoCaption is the only mobile app that offers real-time captioning of phone calls through live stenographers and automated speech recognition software. Our real-time captioning technology makes phone calls easy and accessible for the deaf and hard-of-hearing community. Funded by the FCC, InnoCaption is offered at no cost to eligible users in the United States. For more information, visit www.innocaption.com or email us at support@innocaption.com.
categories: Deaf & Hard of Hearing; Telecommunication Devices

name: Inspiration
description: Simple to use but powerful in its impact on users, Inspiration is the ideal visual thinking tool for easily creating mind maps, concept maps, graphic organizers, outlines, and presentations! Our visual mapping tool makes it easy to quickly capture and visually organize your ideas to communicate concepts and strengthen understanding. Transfer your visual diagram to a written outline in just one click, perfect for structuring writing plans for papers and reports.

name: International Association of Accessibility Professionals (IAAP)
description: The mission of the International Association of Accessibility Professionals (IAAP), a division of the Global Initiative for Inclusive ICT’s (G3ict), is to define, promote, improve, and diversify the accessibility profession globally through certification, education, and networking to enable the creation of accessible products, content, services, and environments.

name: Inventivio
description: The new Tactonom Reader enables blind people to access, understand, and learn with graphics independently. The interactive system is especially suited for education because it allows users to work with the same learning formats as sighted people. This is achieved by augmenting tactile information with additional audio explanations about what you sense under your fingertips. Discover how the Tactonom Reader enables blind people to realize their full educational potential.

name: Irie-AT Inc.
description: Irie-AT joins ViewPlus Technologies for CSUN 2024! Visit us for the latest video magnification, braille embossers, tactile graphics solutions, scan/read devices, and more! Highlights include NEW VP Rogue embossers, NEW CloverNote magnifier, IRIE braille embosser, and unique tactile graphics solutions.
categories: Blind/Low Vision; Daily Living; Educational Software; Learning Disabilities; Software

name: JW.ORG
description: JW.ORG, the world's largest Bible educational website, makes spirituality accessible with RTF, Notetaker, MP3, and screen readers. Free downloads are available in over 1000 languages, with braille literature in over 40. Age-appropriate large print, audio, and braille items are provided without charge, as we encourage all to have a better quality of life.
categories: Blind/Low Vision; Daily Living; Deaf & Hard of Hearing; Educational Software; Other

name: Kalogon, Inc
description: Kalogon is on a mission to empower people to live an active, seated lives by creating medically validated, innovative solutions that prevent pressure injuries & increase comfort. By prioritizing empathy-driven design, we develop dynamic, cutting-edge seating solutions that enhance mobility, help alleviate discomfort, & improve overall quality of life for those who spend an extended time sitting. Visit us to learn more about our patent-pending technology, and products Orbiter and Booster.

name: Kurzweil Education
description: At Kurzweil Education, we believe every learner deserves the opportunity to unlock their potential. Our literacy technology offers multi-sensory approaches to reading, comprehension, study skills, writing, and test-taking so everyone can become independent, confident learners who can achieve rigorous academic goals.

name: Level Access
description: Level Access empowers organizations with advanced software, expert-managed services, and training solutions to create accessible and legally compliant digital experiences. Level Access' Technology provides advanced testing, monitoring, and reporting access. Managed services include manual evaluation, remediation guidance, training and enablement, in-house legal support, and accessibility program management.

name: Lightvision
description: We provide low-vision individuals with an AI video analysis-based bus route number guidance solution. We aim to enhance the mobility rights of low-vision individuals through AI technology. Bus route number detection and recognition capability (front, side, and rear recognition). Guidance features based on voice, text, and vibration. GPS-based destination and bus route number search functionality. Customized UI/UX app for individuals with low vision
categories: Assistive Mobility; Blind/Low Vision; Daily Living; Software

name: LS&S, LLC
description: LS&S is a national catalog company selling products for the visually impaired and hard of hearing. Our products range from simple yet crucial daily living aids, such as talking watches and low-vision calendars, to complex electronic magnification products. We have been in business for over 35 years and bring a strong depth of product knowledge to all of our customer contacts. Our sales and customer service staff is often considered the best in the business.
categories: Blind/Low Vision; Daily Living; Telecommunication Devices

name: LVI Low Vision International
description: LVI Low Vision International designs, develops, and produces low-vision products with high reliability, simplicity, and serviceability standards. With over 40 years in the industry, LVI works towards even more innovative solutions that will make every day easier for people with visual impairments.

name: MoreDream Inc.
description: Unlocking limitless possibilities for the visually impaired, our SMARTIO series redefines accessibility. MoreDream Inc. pioneers a social revolution, empowering through innovation. Bridging the digital divide is our mission, elevating lives and ensuring sustainable progress for the marginalized. We champion Braille literacy, job creation, gender parity, local economic resurgence, and an enriched life for the visually impaired.
categories: Alternative Input/Keyboard Devices; Blind/Low Vision; Educational Software; Learning Disabilities; Software

name: Morphic - Trace Center - Raising the Floor
description: Free, open-source AT-on-Demand is a new utility allowing AT users to use their AT on ANY computer at school, the library, the community center, or even friends and family computers. Companion (also free) Morphic provides easier access to built-in features and makes it easier for seniors and those who don't use AT. Installs in seconds. Install on all computers at the organization for free. (Total privacy - supported by gifts and grants.)
categories: AAC; Alternative Input/Keyboard Devices; Blind/Low Vision; Cognitive; Daily Living; Educational Software; Learning Disabilities; Other; Software

name: MVI Co., Ltd.
description: Our products, Voice-Go and MVISION both have been developed for the visually impaired who have difficulties in communicating. The wearable Voice-Go with the universal design has been created. For Voice-Go, It is activating the perception of objects and converting it into sound to transmit. Regarding MVISION, it is based on the OCR(Optical Character Recognition) technology for magnifying the character on the paper. This device has been developed of the low vision impaired.

name: Nclude.ai
description: End-to-end voice-powered task completion and assistance using the latest in generative AI. Learn more about our consumer products that help people with disabilities access digital spaces using voice (including websites and apps that are otherwise inaccessible), as well as our business offerings that help businesses convert their websites and apps into conversational experiences.
categories: Agencies/Services; Blind/Low Vision; Cognitive; Daily Living; Software

name: Nelnet
description:
For over 30 years, Nelnet has operated with the core value of creating superior customer experiences for everyone.
Our dedicated accessibility experts use a combination of manual testing, automated technology, and audits designed specifically for your site and data. We offer flexible suite services to meet your needs, including accessibility assessment, mobile, web, and document repair and remediation, and training and coaching.
categories: Agencies/Services; Blind/Low Vision; Deaf & Hard of Hearing; Other; Software

name: NewHaptics
description: NewHaptics is a manufacturer of refreshable braille technology.

name: NorthState Assistive Technology
description: NorthState Assistive Technology is dedicated to enhancing the independence of blind, vision-impaired, and cognitively challenged individuals through adaptive technology. We proudly represent several different manufacturers to provide the best options available for our clients. In addition, we specialize in customized system solutions, like our Revolution, Revo2Go, and iGoPro, that provide users with the best technology available streamlined to maximize their effectiveness.
categories: Alternative Input/Keyboard Devices; Blind/Low Vision; Daily Living; Software

name: School Health Corporation
description: Our selection of special education products serves education professionals, Occupational Therapists, Speech Language Pathologists, Physical Therapists, Behavior Support Professionals, and more. We provide a superior product selection, personalized service and training, and cost-effective solutions to help you support your students so they can reach their developmental goals.
categories: AAC; Alternative Input/Keyboard Devices; Assistive Mobility; Blind/Low Vision; Cognitive; Daily Living; Deaf & Hard of Hearing; Educational Software; Learning Disabilities

name: ONECOM CO., LTD.
description: FINTIN V1, a smartphone QWERTY communicator, is designed for the visually impaired. It diverges from conventional Braille devices by employing a regular QWERTY layout, which enhances ease of learning and broadens compatibility. The device is a CES 2024 Best Of Innovation Award Winner in the Mobile Devices, Accessories & Apps category and recognized as an Innovation Honoree in the Computer Peripherals & Accessories category.
categories: Alternative Input/Keyboard Devices; Blind/Low Vision; Daily Living

name: OpenText
description: OpenText Enterprise Document Accessibility solutions are used by financial institutions, healthcare providers, utilities, public sector agencies, and many other organizations to capture and transform high-volume print streams or document compositions, such as invoices, monthly banking, credit card, explanation of benefits, or investment statements into PDF documents that are accessible using assistive technology for visually-impaired customers.

name: Orbit Research
description: Orbit Research develops and manufactures braille, tactile, and educational products for people with visual impairments. Our products include the revolutionary Orbit Reader family of affordable braille displays with signage quality braille, the Orbit Slate family of multi-line braille displays, the Graphiti family of Interactive Tactile Graphics Displays, the Orion family of calculators, the Orbit Speak braille notetaker, the Optima Braille Laptop Computer, the Magna family of digital magnifiers.
categories: Alternative Input/Keyboard Devices; Blind/Low Vision; Daily Living; Educational Software; Learning Disabilities; Software; Telecommunication Devices

name: OrCam
description: OrCam delivers increased independence to people who are blind, visually impaired, or have reading challenges - empowering them to study, work, and throughout their daily activities. OrCam MyEye is a wearable with a smart camera that attaches to an eyeglass frame, enabling you to read, recognize faces, identify products, and more. OrCam Read is a handheld device with an intuitive point-and-click operation that seamlessly reads text aloud from any printed surface or digital screen.
categories: Blind/Low Vision; Daily Living; Learning Disabilities

name: Overflow Biz, Inc.
description: We showcase VERSA SLATE, a paperless and refreshable braille slate with an optional paper braille kit. Our indigenous software for low vision, FLOWY, is an all-in-one AI video magnifier & reader software and Instant Flowy Zoom app in one smart purchase. FLOWY offers AI hand gesture commands fully integrated with OCR & TTS and much more. It is the most practical and intuitive solution for your home and office. Go with the flow, FLOWY!
categories: Blind/Low Vision; Daily Living; Educational Software; Software

name: Paige Braille Ltd.
description: To write Braille, people currently emboss paper using a braille writer, which has sold 400,000 units worldwide. However, the information produced cannot be easily stored, shared, or understood by people who cannot read Braille. We aim to change this. We are building Paige Connect, an affordable adaptation for existing Braille writers, enabling collaboration between blind and sighted people.
categories: Alternative Input/Keyboard Devices; Blind/Low Vision; Educational Software; Software

name: Patriot Vision Industries LLC
description: Patriot Vision Industries - we design, manufacture, and distribute our exclusive Patriot brand of unique adaptive equipment for the visually challenged industry. The renowned Patriot brand is amazing whether stand-alone, tablet-based, handheld, or wearable. 800-364-1610.

name: Pope Tech
description: Pope Tech provides website accessibility scanning and reporting. We help simplify your accessibility efforts across your portfolio of websites. Pope Tech also has Canvas LMS course accessibility products to help everyone (admins and instructors) create more accessible Canvas courses. The WAVE accessibility engine powers all Pope Tech Products.

name: Procter & Gamble
description: P&G's brands are trusted in millions of living rooms, kitchens, laundry rooms, and bathrooms—and have been passed down from generation to generation. We are the people behind the brands you trust, and we're committed to enhancing our products with inclusive features to make people's lives better in small but meaningful ways every day. We've got some game-changing products for you to interact with. Say hello and experience our products yourself - we'd love your feedback.
categories: Blind/Low Vision; Cognitive; Daily Living; Deaf & Hard of Hearing; Learning Disabilities

name: QD Laser, Inc.
description: QD Laser, Inc. is a company that has been at the forefront of advanced laser technology and has developed the RETISSA series utilizing retinal projection technology. This retinal projection technology (VISIRIUM Technology) delivers bright and clear images to the entire retina without depending on the focus adjustment function. We are showcasing the RETISSA ON HAND, which is a video magnifier, and the RETISSA NEOVIEWER, which serves as a viewfinder.

name: Quality of Life Plus Program
description: We partner with college engineering students who design and build assistive technology for disabled veterans and first responders.

name: RealThing Ai
description: RealThing AI specializes in voice interface solutions for the Blind and Visually Impaired. The RealSAM Pocket is a phone equipped with our voice interface technology. With this phone, you can easily make calls or send messages, add contacts using your voice, and access book libraries & other content. It includes tools such as OCR, object recognition, video magnifier, direct integration with Be My Eyes, location services, and information. RealSAM is unlocked and works with any US mobile carriers.
categories: Blind/Low Vision; Daily Living; Telecommunication Devices

name: ReBokeh Vision Technologies Inc.
description: ReBokeh is making organizations and employers more accessible with our full-service vision assistance tool. Using location technology, we enable ReBokeh in your space, allowing each person to choose how they see the world through our flagship smartphone app. ReBokeh allows users to adjust contrast, brightness, color, and more in real time, all from their devices.

name: Reinecker Vision GmbH
description: Reinecker Vision is a family-owned German company with more than 50 years of experience developing, producing, selling, and servicing optoelectronic video magnifiers. We have become the European leader in low-vision technology. The innovative new mezzo-series proves again that Reinecker Vision is one step ahead. Visit us and be inspired by the difference we are offering. We offer a full range of Low Vision products and are happy to service you.

name: Rhonda Weiss Center for Accessible IDEA Data
description: The Rhonda Weiss Center for Accessible IDEA Data improves state capacity to report Part B and Part C Individuals with Disabilities Education Act (IDEA) data in accessible formats.
categories: Agencies/Services; Alternative Input/Keyboard Devices; Blind/Low Vision; Cognitive; Learning Disabilities; Software

name: RJ Cooper & Associates, Inc.
description: Access hardware and software for significantly disabled persons, for computers, tablets, and smartphones. RJ helped create our AT field in 1983, exhibiting at the very first CSUN Conference at the university!
categories: AAC; Alternative Input/Keyboard Devices; Blind/Low Vision; Educational Software; Other; Software

name: Royal National Institute of Blind People
description: RNIB, the Royal National Institute of Blind People, is the UK's leading sight loss charity. RNIB provides a range of exclusive products that can help Blind and Partially sighted people live independently. RNIB is proud to launch our latest artificial Intelligence technology product, ARx AI. The ARx AI headset is an AI-enabled talking camera for the blind and partially sighted community. ARx AI, The World made accessible, hands-free.
categories: Assistive Mobility; Blind/Low Vision; Daily Living; Educational Software; Telecommunication Devices

name: Salesforce
description: At Salesforce, we recognize the powerful insights that users with disabilities bring to our product development process, and we’re committed to creating products that work for everyone.

name: School Health Corporation
description: Our selection of special education products serves education professionals, Occupational Therapists, Speech Language Pathologists, Physical Therapists, Behavior Support Professionals, and more. We provide a superior product selection, personalized service and training, and cost-effective solutions to help you support your students so they can reach their developmental goals.
categories: AAC; Alternative Input/Keyboard Devices; Assistive Mobility; Blind/Low Vision; Cognitive; Daily Living; Deaf & Hard of Hearing; Educational Software; Learning Disabilities

name: sComm
description: sComm is the creator of the UbiDuo 3. The UbiDuo 3 communication device enables a deaf/hard of hearing and hearing person to communicate face-to-face without an interpreter. The UbiDuo 3 allows deaf or hard-of-hearing people to converse with hearing people anywhere, anytime, in professional or social settings. Come check out the new UbiDuo 3 SGD, approved for Medicare/Medicaid and private insurance.
categories: AAC; Alternative Input/Keyboard Devices; Cognitive; Daily Living; Deaf & Hard of Hearing

name: Awarewolf Gear
description:
Originally designed for the backcountry trails of Sedona, Arizona, the All-Terrain Cane (ATC) excels everywhere life takes you. Whether you're taking a stroll down the block, at the beach, on snowy sidewalks, your grandchild's playground, or on the trails, the ATC gives you the power to explore where other mobility canes wouldn't dream to go. The ATC features traditional white and red reflective materials as users are accustomed to, and much more.
Introducing the Urban Xplorer series for 2024.
categories: Assistive Mobility; Blind/Low Vision; Daily Living

name: Seleste
description: Seleste makes thin, affordable and powerful smart glasses to give people with vision loss back their independence. We have a mic, speaker and a camera in the middle of the glasses that allow you to read text, describe your environment and more. It can do all this through our assistant that answers questions like "Do these socks match? Where is my wallet? Is this car a Toyota Camry? "Can you describe the artwork as I walk around this art gallery?" You can also save text to send to your computer

name: SENSEE Inc.
description: Touch the World & See the Unseen. The founder of SENSEE is a member of a family with visual impairments; therefore, SENSEE is based on their understanding of them. We aim to provide equal opportunities to enjoy together, whether or not their friends and family members have a disability. Imagine the visually impaired kids with parents and visually impaired parents with kids enjoying together.
categories: Blind/Low Vision; Daily Living; Educational Software; Learning Disabilities; Other; Software

name: Silktide
description: Silktide's accessibility platform helps websites comply with the latest WCAG 2.1 accessibility standard across desktop and mobile devices. We also offer a range of free tools for website accessibility, including simulators for blindness, dyslexia, and many other cognitive and sensory impairments. Founded in 2001, Silktide helps thousands of customers analyze and improve tens of millions of websites annually.

name: Solar Grove Solutions
description: Solar Grove Solutions is the foremost provider of globally accessible tech solutions to bridge the language gaps with an easy-to-use, rugged, low-cost, accessible solar-powered audio player. The Vine C1 eReader is a breakthrough for the blind and visually impaired globally. Solar Grove Solutions also launches its rugged solar rechargeable Leaf tablet, the world's loudest 10-inch touch screen secure tablet for accessible learning for Deaf and low hearing. Contact us at: info@solargrove.solutions
categories: Blind/Low Vision; Daily Living; Deaf & Hard of Hearing; Learning Disabilities; Other

name: Sony North America
description: We leverage creativity and technology to enhance the accessibility of our products, services, and experiences. Through our commitment to an inclusive future, we aim to create a world where everyone belongs. We strive to enable and empower individuals of all abilities to share Kando (emotion). For more accessibility information, feel free to contact us at APO-USA@am.sony.com.

name: Sorenson
description: Sorenson is one of the world’s leading language services providers and the world’s leading provider of communication tools for Deaf and hard-of-hearing people. We combine patented technology with human-centric services to connect signed and spoken languages: captioning and video relay services, over-video and in-person sign language and spoken language interpreting, real-time event captioning services, and post-production language services.

name: Splash Box Marketing
description: We are a trusted provider of ADA compliance accessibility solutions, including 508 remediations of PDF, Word, Excel, and PPT documents, website consulting, large print documents, and video and audio file creation. A proud recipient of the 2023 Gold International Stevie Award for Technology Department of the Year and Technology Executive of the Year because of our dedication to making the internet more accessible for the hard of sight. It's not just the law; it's the right thing to do.

name: StrapTech
description: Ara is an innovative wearable device designed for the visually impaired, aiming to enhance navigation through advanced sensor technology. It detects obstacles at head level, mid-level, and low level using ultrasonic and lidar sensors, providing real-time haptic feedback for safe and confident movement. Ideally used in conjunction with a white cane, Ara adapts to your preferred mobility method, ensuring seamless integration into your daily life.
categories: Assistive Mobility; Blind/Low Vision; Daily Living

name: Tandem Design Co.
description: At the heart of Tandem Design Co lies a simple but profound belief: the world is richer when it's more colorful and inclusive. By infusing this philosophy into everyday items, we're not just designing products—we're designing a brighter, more inclusive future for everyone.

name: The Dayle McIntosh Center for the Disabled
description: The Dayle McIntosh Center (DMC) is a nonprofit organization known as a Center for Independent Living. We are dedicated to empowering individuals with disabilities and older adults throughout Orange County to break down barriers that prevent them from achieving self-sufficiency. Mission Statement: Access and equity by and for people with disabilities and older adults. For information on our free services, visit www.daylemc.org
categories: Agencies/Services; Blind/Low Vision; Daily Living; Deaf & Hard of Hearing

name: The Genesis Group Inc
description: The Genesis Group is a travel healthcare and education staffing company providing school districts and healthcare facilities with expereicned qualified staff across the country.
categories: AAC; Agencies/Services; Alternative Input/Keyboard Devices; Blind/Low Vision; Deaf & Hard of Hearing; Educational Software; Learning Disabilities; Telecommunication Devices

name: The National Institutes of Health
description: The NIH, under the U.S. Dept. of Health and Human Services, conducts vital medical research, comprising 27 Institutes and Centers, each with distinct focuses. Most receive congressional funding and manage their budgets. NIH aims to advance understanding of living systems to improve health and well-being. Explore career opportunities at https://hr.nih.gov/jobs.

name: Thinkerbell Labs Pvt. Ltd.
description: Thinkerbell Labs is an EdTech organization building innovative, accessible solutions that help improve learning outcomes for the visually impaired. The flagship product, Annie, is The World's First Self-Learning Braille Literacy device that helps the visually impaired learn to read, write, and type in Braille through interactive audio-guided content in vernacular languages.

name: Time Timer LLC
description: Time Timer® is an innovative, simple time management tool designed to “show” the passage of time through the use of a colored red disk. As time elapses, the colored disk disappears—making common routines at work, school, or home stress-free and enjoyable. Today, the award-winning and research-proven Time Timer family of products helps individuals of all ages and abilities worldwide better visualize and manage the passage of time.

name: Touch Graphics, Inc.
description: At CSUN 2024 Touch Graphics will introduce T3 Creator, a new authoring system that will allow anyone to produce their own custom audio-tactile graphics. Booth staff will demonstrate how to place tactile overlays on a T3 Tablet, then define touch-responsive zones by tracing around them with your finger, and recording your own descriptions.

name: TrySight Inc.
description: TrySight enhances accessibility with Desktop Magnifiers, Wearables, Portable Cameras, and Multitasking devices, empowering the visually impaired to read, navigate, learn, and perform daily activities powered by cutting-edge technology.

name: Turtleback
description: Our mission is to design and manufacture the most ergonomic, protective, and durable carry cases for low-vision aid devices. We do this through our relentless pursuit of customer feedback and satisfaction. We've been manufacturing cases for low-vision aid devices for over 20 years. Previously resold under different brands, most were unaware of Turtleback's' role in making carry cases for low-vision devices. As such, we are re-introducing ourselves so they can continue to enjoy our products.

name: U.S. Department of State
description: The U.S. Department of State represents the U.S. at more than 270 diplomatic locations worldwide, including embassies, consulates, and missions to international organizations. The Office of Accessibility and Accommodations (OAA) provides centralized disability services & expertise to advance the Department of State's mission worldwide. OAA provides Schedule A hiring, reasonable accommodation services, assistive technology, Sign Language Interpreters, Section 508, & Video Captioning.

name: Venngage, Inc.
description: Venngage is a DIY storytelling platform that helps users create accessible designs like infographics, presentations, brochures, etc.

name: Verbit
description: Verbit is a dedicated partner in creating inclusive learning environments by offering industry-standard accessibility solutions such as captioning, transcription, audio description, and translation.
categories: Agencies/Services; Blind/Low Vision; Educational Software; Learning Disabilities

name: AYES
description:
AYES provides an AI application, called oko, that helps visually impaired people navigate their environment safely and independently. Our oko app makes every traffic light accessible by detecting the traffic light and bringing that information back to the user using audio and haptic feedback.
Our oko app has helped cross more than 2 million streets to date and is growing rapidly. Join our booth 619 to learn more about the future of the oko app.

name: Vispero
description: Vispero is the world's leading assistive technology provider for the visually impaired. We have a long history of developing and providing innovative solutions for blind and low-vision individuals that help them reach their full potential. At Vispero, we inspire hope, determination, and independence through our family of brands: Freedom Scientific, Enhanced Vision, Optelec, and TPGi.
categories: Agencies/Services; Alternative Input/Keyboard Devices; Blind/Low Vision; Educational Software; Learning Disabilities; Software

name: Wayfinder Family Services
description: Wayfinder provides services for transition-age youth and adults who are blind or visually impaired. Wayfinder’s Davidson Program for Independence is a comprehensive residential program. Training in Braille, independent living skills, assistive technology, orientation, and mobility allow graduates to reach their greatest levels of independence. Other programs include Transition Services workshops and Employment Services. All services are provided in partnership with DOR.

name: WeWALK Limited
description: WeWALK is a smart cane for the blind that detects obstacles at the head and chest level and warns the user. It supports voice interaction with its microphone, speaker, and touchpad. Paired with smartphones, users can take clockwise, turn-by-turn navigation through their cane. It can also gain new features through software updates. The visually impaired can access public transportation info through their cane. WeWALK was selected as one of the best inventions of 2019 by TIME Magazine.
categories: Assistive Mobility; Blind/Low Vision; Daily Living; Other; Software

name: XR Navigation
description: XR Navigation presents our first product, Audiom, designed to make geography accessible to everyone, including people with visual impairments, mobility issues, and others who struggle with maps. Audiom is a map component that presents spatial information in three ways: visually, auditorily, and tactually via text and braille. The map can be embedded on a website or in any app. Audiom is the only legal mapping solution and is compliant with the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines AAA.

name: Zoomax Technology Co., Limited
description: Zoomax, a Rejoin Solutions subsidiary, is a developer and manufacturer of technology dedicated to creating independence for people with vision impairments. Offering wearable and a full line of handheld, portable and desktop video magnifiers, the Zoomax R&D team specializes in innovative, efficient, and robust hardware design. With innovations like developing our own HD camera, Zoomax delivers superior quality at affordable prices.
categories: Assistive Mobility; Blind/Low Vision; Daily Living

name: ZP Better Together
description: Purple Communications is dedicated to delivering the highest-quality and most innovative communication services to meet the unique needs of each Deaf and hard-of-hearing individual.
categories: Deaf & Hard of Hearing; Software; Telecommunication Devices
